name: Swarm
flavor: The discipline of overwhelming numbers: a Doppelganger who stops treating a Clone as one spare body and starts fielding a crowd, letting sheer duplication wear a target down from every angle at once.

features.name: Overflowing Reflections
features.description: Your reflections multiply more readily than most. Your Clone cap increases by 1 (this stacks with the increase Hall of Mirrors grants at 5th level). Additionally, once per combat, when you use your bonus action to create a Clone, you may create a second Clone at the same time, provided doing so would not exceed your (increased) Clone cap; both appear following the normal rules for Clone creation, in unoccupied spaces you can see within range.

features.name: Overwhelming Numbers
features.description: You press your numbers from every angle at once — and then take the legs out. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack while one of your Clones is within 5 feet of it, two things happen: you deal an extra [[1d4]] damage of the weapon's damage type as the creature flinches from a second angle it can't track, and the Clone sweeps in at the same instant, forcing the target to succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your {{save DC|8 + proficiency bonus + Constitution modifier}} or be knocked prone. Both happen at most once per turn no matter how many Clones are adjacent, and this combines with the advantage granted by Flanking Reflection.

meta.save: Con save vs your DC (or prone)

features.name: One-Man Crowd
features.description: Why blend into a crowd when you can bring your own? Outside combat, your Clones can wear faces that aren't yours — distinct strangers rather than copies — and can mill about within 60 feet of you: enough to fake a gathering, an entourage, a queue, or a very busy alibi. You have advantage on ability checks made to blend into, start, or read a crowd, and you know instantly when one of your Clones is destroyed, and where it was.
features.sheetSummary: Out of combat, Clones can look like distinct strangers and mill within 60 ft (fake crowds, entourages, alibis). Advantage on checks to blend into/start/read crowds. Know instantly when and where a Clone is destroyed.
